req.title: KYC refresh
req.statement: A customer shall complete a KYC refresh every 365 days; accounts beyond refresh window shall be restricted to inbound transactions only.

doc.reasoning: KYC refresh is a time-gated compliance control. Testing spans three dimensions: (1) successful refresh within the 365-day window (EP positive path), (2) rejection when compliance fails (NEG), and (3) boundary conditions at refresh window edges (BVA). State transitions (STC) validate the customer.verified flag lifecycle. Decision table logic covers authenticated state + submission validity combinations.

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_valid_submission_within_window
actions: POST /kyc/refresh with valid form data (name, address, dob, tax_id)
tests.executable:
import requests
import time
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def test_kyc_refresh_valid_submission_within_window():
    auth_token = <get_auth_token_fixture>
    customer_id = <customer_id_fixture>
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}"}
    payload = {
        "name": "John Doe",
        "address": "123 Main St",
        "dob": "1990-01-15",
        "tax_id": "12-3456789"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"http://banking-api/kyc/refresh",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    data = resp.json()
    assert data["customer"]["verified"] is True
    assert "kyc_refresh_ts" in data["customer"]
    assert data.get("account_restriction") is None

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_compliance_failure_returns_403
preconditions: submission form contains invalid/incomplete compliance data
e.g. tax_id fails format validation or name contains prohibited characters
actions: capture HTTP response code and error message
trace: R-FIN-015: Error pathways: 403 when submission fails compliance
Negative path: rejection on compliance violation
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_kyc_refresh_compliance_failure_returns_403():
    auth_token = <get_auth_token_fixture>
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}"}
    payload = {
        "name": "Invalid@Name#",
        "address": "123 Main St",
        "dob": "1990-01-15",
        "tax_id": "INVALID"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"http://banking-api/kyc/refresh",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    data = resp.json()
    assert data.get("error", {}).get("code") == "COMPLIANCE_FAILURE"
    assert "kyc_refresh_ts" not in data.get("customer", {})

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_account_restricted_beyond_window
trace: R-FIN-015: accounts beyond refresh window shall be restricted to inbound transactions only
tests.executable:
import requests
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def test_kyc_refresh_account_restricted_beyond_window():
    auth_token = <get_auth_token_fixture>
    customer_id = <customer_id_fixture>
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {auth_token}"}
    <set_customer_kyc_last_refresh_to_400_days_ago>
    <set_account_restriction_to_inbound_only>
    payload = {
        "name": "Robert Brown",
        "address": "789 Pine Rd",
        "dob": "1992-07-10",
        "tax_id": "55-1234567"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"http://banking-api/kyc/refresh",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    data = resp.json()
    assert data["customer"]["verified"] is True
    assert data.get("account_restriction") is None

tests.name: test_kyc_refresh_unauthenticated_denied
preconditions: request lacks valid authentication token or session
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_kyc_refresh_unauthenticated_denied():
    payload = {
        "name": "Test User",
        "address": "123 Main St",
        "dob": "1990-01-15",
        "tax_id": "12-3456789"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"http://banking-api/kyc/refresh",
        json=payload
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 401
    data = resp.json()
    assert data.get("error", {}).get("code") == "UNAUTHENTICATED"
